content.text:
<user_query>
Install the VSIX extension from this path on Windows:
c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix

Steps:
1. Verify the file exists
2. Find if `cursor` or `code` CLI is available (common paths on Windows: %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\cursor\Cursor.exe, AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd)
3. Run: cursor --install-extension "c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix" OR code --install-extension with same path
4. If cursor CLI not in PATH, try full path to Cursor.exe with --install-extension flag
5. Report exact command run, exit code, and full stdout/stderr output

Return all output verbatim.
</user_query>

Verifying the VSIX file and locating the Cursor or VS Code CLI for installation.

[REDACTED]

input.command: Test-Path "c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix"; if (Test-Path "c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix") { Get-Item "c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix" | Format-List FullName, Length, LastWriteTime }
input.description: Verify VSIX file exists and show metadata

input.command:
$paths = @(
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\cursor\Cursor.exe",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd",
  "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd"
); foreach ($p in $paths) { Write-Host "CHECK: $p -> $(Test-Path $p)" }; Write-Host "--- which ---"; Get-Command cursor -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Format-List; Get-Command code -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Format-List
input.description: Check common Cursor/VS Code CLI paths

input.final_summary:
The command runner in this subagent session did not execute shell commands (every invocation returned exit code 0, empty stdout/stderr, and ~0 ms duration, including a test that should exit 42). Because of that, I could not verify the VSIX on disk or capture real install output here.

**What was attempted (intended exact flow):**
1. `Test-Path` / `dir` on `c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix`
2. Probe CLI at `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd`, `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\cursor\Cursor.exe`, `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd`
3. Install via first available:
   - `cursor --install-extension "c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix"`
   - or `code --install-extension` with the same path
   - or full path to `Cursor.exe` with `--install-extension`

**Run this locally in PowerShell or CMD and paste the full output back for a verbatim report:**

```powershell
$vsix = 'c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix'
Write-Host "File exists:" (Test-Path -LiteralPath $vsix)
$cli = @(
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\cursor\Cursor.exe",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd"
) | Where-Object { Test-Path $_ } | Select-Object -First 1
if (-not $cli) { throw 'No cursor/code CLI found' }
Write-Host "Using:" $cli
& $cli --install-extension $vsix 2>&1 | Tee-Object -Variable out
Write-Host "Exit code:" $LASTEXITCODE
$out
```

A longer install script may also have written logs (if anything ran outside the stub): `C:\Users\Administrator\.cursor\projects\empty-window\install-vsix-result.txt` plus `.stdout` / `.stderr` siblings.

content.text:
## Result

**Shell execution in this subagent session did not run real commands.** Every `Shell` call returned:

- **Exit code:** `0`
- **stdout/stderr:** *(empty)*
- **Duration:** ~`0 ms` *(including a test that should have exited `42`)*

So I **cannot** give you verified file existence, the exact command that ran on your machine, or verbatim install output from this environment.

---

## Commands intended (run these on your machine)

**Step 1 – verify file**

```powershell
Test-Path -LiteralPath 'c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix'
Get-Item -LiteralPath 'c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Format-List FullName, Length
```

**Step 2 – find CLI**

```powershell
@(
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\cursor\Cursor.exe",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd",
  "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd"
) | ForEach-Object { "$_ -> $(Test-Path $_)" }
where.exe cursor 2>&1
where.exe code 2>&1
```

**Step 3–4 – install (first match wins)**

```cmd
"%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd" --install-extension "c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix"
```

or:

```cmd
"%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\cursor\Cursor.exe" --install-extension "c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix"
```

or:

```cmd
"%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd" --install-extension "c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix"
```

**One-shot PowerShell (prints exit code and all output):**

```powershell
$vsix = 'c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix'
Write-Host "File exists:" (Test-Path -LiteralPath $vsix)
$cli = @(
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\cursor\Cursor.exe",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd"
) | Where-Object { Test-Path $_ } | Select-Object -First 1
if (-not $cli) { Write-Error 'No cursor/code CLI found'; exit 1 }
Write-Host "Exact command:" "$cli --install-extension `"$vsix`""
& $cli --install-extension $vsix 2>&1 | Tee-Object -Variable out
Write-Host "Exit code:" $LASTEXITCODE
$out
```

---

## Optional log files (if a background script ran)

- `C:\Users\Administrator\.cursor\projects\empty-window\install-vsix-result.txt`
- `C:\Users\Administrator\.cursor\projects\empty-window\install-vsix-result.txt.stdout`
- `C:\Users\Administrator\.cursor\projects\empty-window\install-vsix-result.txt.stderr`
